items.text: DurabilityError enum defined in crates/liminal/src/durability/error.rs with variants for haematite errors, sequence conflicts, cursor regression, dedup collisions, and configuration errors.
DurabilityError implements std::error::Error via thiserror with From conversions for all haematite error types.

DurabilityConfig struct defined in crates/liminal/src/durability/config.rs with fields for durability mode (ephemeral, durable, durable-dedup, durable-conversation), partition count, dedup TTL, and checkpoint policy.
DurableStore trait defined in crates/liminal/src/durability/store.rs with methods mapping to haematite's append, read_from, cas, and scan operations.
HaematiteStore struct implements DurableStore by delegating to haematite's EventStore API.
durability/mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports. No logic.
Durable channel appends each published message to haematite via append(channel_id + partition, envelope, expected_seq) before acknowledging.
Message envelope contains payload, causal context, timestamp, publisher identity, and idempotency key (if provided).
Partition routing derives partition index from the channel's partition key function applied to the message.
Each partition maintains an independent monotonic sequence number tracked by the durable channel.
Sequence conflict on append (expected_seq mismatch) returns a DurabilityError::SequenceConflict, never retries silently.
Consumer cursor persists offset via cas(consumer_id + partition, old_offset, new_offset) to haematite.
cas failure (old_offset does not match stored value) returns DurabilityError::CursorRegression, never overwrites.
On consumer restart, cursor is read from haematite and processing resumes from the stored offset.
Checkpoint policy supports per-message, per-batch, and explicit-flush modes configured in DurabilityConfig.
replay_from(partition, offset) returns an ordered iterator of messages from the given offset to the partition head.
Replay respects causal ordering within a partition: messages are returned in sequence number order.
Replay of an empty range (offset equals partition head) returns an empty iterator, not an error.
Dedup cache stores idempotency key to processing receipt mapping in haematite via append(dedup_namespace + key_hash, receipt, expected_seq).
Duplicate publish with an existing idempotency key that has a receipt returns the stored receipt without re-delivering the message.
Duplicate publish with an existing idempotency key that has no receipt yet (processing in-flight) defers delivery per the channel's backpressure policy.
Background sweep removes dedup entries older than the configured TTL.
TTL expiry never removes an entry younger than the configured TTL, accounting for up to 1 second of clock skew.
Processing receipt is an opaque bytes value provided by the consumer after successful processing.
Receipt is stored against the idempotency key in the dedup cache, making the entry complete.
Durable conversation appends each state transition as an event via append(conversation_id, event, expected_seq) to haematite.
Event types include: message_received, processing_started, step_completed (with output), processing_finished, and error_occurred.
On restart, conversation actor replays its event log from haematite and reconstructs its processing state.
Re-delivery of an already-processed message is detected by checking the event log and results in a no-op skip.
Concurrent conversation instances are detected via expected_seq conflict on append; only one instance proceeds.
Crash recovery for durable channels reconstructs partition sequence numbers from haematite's stored log.
Crash recovery for durable conversations replays the event log and resumes from the last completed step.
Crash recovery for consumer cursors reads the persisted offset and replays messages from that point.
Replaying the same event log twice produces identical conversation state (idempotent replay).
Ephemeral channels do not hold a DurableStore reference. The type system prevents accidental persistence calls.
Ephemeral channels perform zero haematite operations: no append, no read, no cas.
